title: micropsis dasycarpa
intro: Micropsis dasycarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the annual or biennial plant that belongs to the genus Micropsis. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other secondary metabolites, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Micropsis dasycarpa include its potential use in the treatment of various conditions, such as fever, pain, and infections, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Micropsis dasycarpa are typically made from its leaves and flowers, which can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a powder for medicinal purposes.
